chapter_title.en: Chapter on Performing sa'i While Riding and Sitting Between Safa and Marwah

text: 2852 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلنِّسَاءِ يَطُفْنَ عَلَى اَلْإِبِلِ وَ اَلدَّوَابِّ بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ أَ يُجْزِيهِنَّ أَنْ يَقِفْنَ تَحْتَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ حَيْثُ يَرَيْنَ اَلْبَيْتَ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2852 - Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about women performing the Sa'i (ritual walking) between Safa and Marwah on camels or other riding animals: "Is it sufficient for them to stand beneath Safa and Marwah at a point where they can see the Ka'bah?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
